page: OVHcloud bm-s1 is a bare-metal server in the Metal family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 CPU architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the server is equipped with a 960 GB NVMe SSD, offering high-speed read and write capabilities directly on the physical host. Networking is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no integrated GPUs on this instance.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the bm-s1 is positioned as a memory-optimized or storage-oriented entry-level bare-metal server. The dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed of 4.0 GHz make it suitable for workloads requiring consistent processing power without virtualization overhead. It is appropriate for small-scale databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications that benefit from dedicated hardware resources and local NVMe storage.
description: OVHcloud bm-s1 is a bare-metal server from the Metal family, built on the x86_64 architecture and running under the KVM hypervisor. The system features 4 dedicated vCPUs with a high clock speed of 4.0 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio. Storage is provided by a 960 GB NVMe SSD, ensuring fast local data access. The network configuration delivers a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. Lacking a GPU, this server focuses on raw CPU and memory performance. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated hardware resources make the bm-s1 highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, small database deployments, and caching applications that require predictable performance without virtualization interference.
